title: Domain Names
drDigitalIntro: A domain name is the core of a website's address. In google.com, 'google' is the domain name and '.com' is the extension. .com means commercial, .org means organization, .edu means education. Some sites use country codes: .uk for United Kingdom, .ca for Canada. Domain names are unique — only one website can own google.com. If you want to start a website, you need to buy a domain name.
drDigitalSuccess: You understand how domain names and extensions work!
drDigitalHint: The extension (.com, .org) tells you something about the site.
